meta.name: Silverbrook
meta.description: A small frontier town nestled at the edge of the wild lands. Stone houses with copper roofs surround a cobblestone market square. The road north climbs toward the watchtower and beyond into untamed country.

rooms.name: The Bronze Hart Inn
rooms.description: The common room of The Bronze Hart Inn. A massive stone hearth crackles along the western wall, throwing flickering light across worn oak tables. The smell of roasting meat and woodsmoke fills the air. A staircase climbs to the rooms above; a small bar runs along the back wall. The exit leads north to the market square.

rooms.name: Market Square
rooms.description: The cobbled heart of Silverbrook. Stalls cluster around a stone fountain whose carved stag spits a thin stream of water. Townsfolk haggle over apples, leather goods, and bolts of cloth. A weathered notice board near the fountain bears tacked parchments. Roads lead north up to the gate, east to the smithy, west to the tavern, and south back to the inn.

rooms.name: Old John's Smithy
rooms.description: The forge glows orange under a peaked roof of slate tiles. Hammers, tongs, and blanks of iron line the walls; an anvil sits squarely in the center of a hard-packed earthen floor. The clang of metal-on-metal carries from the back. A rack of finished blades stands near the door. The market square lies west.

rooms.name: The Crooked Stag Tavern
rooms.description: Low ceiling beams stained black from hearthsmoke and time. A long bar runs the length of the room, behind which Bren the tavernkeeper polishes pewter mugs. Locals nurse ales at scarred trestle tables, and a dartboard hangs near the kitchen door. The market square is east.

rooms.name: North Gate
rooms.description: A pair of oaken gates banded in iron stand open in the town's stone wall. A guardhouse leans against the inside of the wall to your right; a torch in a sconce sputters above the doorway. Beyond the threshold the road climbs north toward the watchtower; back south, the market square bustles with life.

rooms.name: Watchtower Overlook
rooms.description: The road ends at the foot of a squat stone watchtower. From the parapet thirty feet above, you can see for miles — pale grass, dark forests, and farther still the jagged shadow of mountains where it's said dragons used to nest. A wooden ladder climbs to the platform. The path back south leads to the gate. A faint deer-trail winds north into the open country.

npcs.name: Old Marta
npcs.title: Innkeeper
npcs.personality: Round-faced and warm. Knows every traveler who's passed through in the last decade, and a fair few who haven't. Treats first-time guests like long-lost children.
lore: A room upstairs is 5 silver a night, with breakfast — sleep here and you'll wake at the inn even if you fall in the wild.
Captain Halvar at the watchtower has been jumpy lately; he says he saw lights moving in the foothills three nights running.
Old John at the smithy lost his apprentice to the wild last spring. Don't ask him about it unless you've got news.
The deer-trail north of the watchtower used to lead to a hill shrine. Hasn't been a pilgrim through here in years.
If you head north and don't come back, the gate captain will note it. She always notes it.

npcs.name: Greta
npcs.title: Fruit Seller
npcs.personality: Cheerful and gossipy. Wears a bright red shawl. Always has a kind word and a sharper opinion.

routines.activity: having an ale and trading rumors

lore: The honey pears come from old man Aldric's orchard south of town. He hasn't been to market in a fortnight, which is unusual.
There's a notice on the board about a wolf taking sheep up by the watchtower road. Captain Vesna pinned it herself.
Old John's apprentice went hunting that wolf, you know. That was last spring. Never came back.
Bren at the tavern says travelers from the north have been talking about strange lights near the old hill shrine.
If you're new in town, stop by the inn first — Marta'll have a room for you, and she knows everything that's worth knowing.

npcs.name: Town Guard
npcs.title: Watchman
npcs.personality: Bored, dutiful, eyes everyone with the slight suspicion that comes from years on the same post.

routines.activity: leaning on a halberd, fighting sleep

lore: Two cattle went missing from the western pastures last week. Wolves, I'd wager. Or worse.
I've been on this post for nine years. Nothing comes through Silverbrook I don't see.
Captain Vesna runs the gate. Don't try to slip past her — she's got eyes like a hawk.
The wild north of here used to be tame country. People hunted it. Doesn't feel that way anymore.
If you're heading out the gate, take a sword. The militia ain't what it used to be.

npcs.name: Old John
npcs.title: Blacksmith
npcs.personality: A weathered Dwarf with soot-blackened hands. Speaks in short bursts. Pretends not to care, secretly takes pride in every blade.
lore: I trained Pell — my apprentice — for six winters. He went hunting a wolf last spring. We never found him.
I forge from Stoneveil iron, mostly. Best ore for two hundred miles. The vein's nearly tapped though.
If you're going north, take the helm at minimum. Skull's heavy stuff to lose.
The shrine on the hill — north of the watchtower — that's old work. Older than anything in the smithy. Older than the town.
My nephew Bren runs the tavern. Tell him I said you're alright and he'll knock a copper off your ale.

npcs.name: Bren
npcs.title: Tavernkeeper
npcs.personality: A wiry man with a long memory. Knows all the local rumors but parts with them grudgingly.
lore: A pair of southern merchants drank here last week. They said the Stoneveil road is choked with bandits since the spring thaw.
Old John drinks here every night after sundown. He'll talk to no one most nights, but he listens.
The hill shrine north of town used to draw pilgrims. Then the lights started, and the pilgrims stopped.
Greta's honey pears come from Aldric's orchard. Aldric hasn't been to market in two weeks. Worth a thought.
Captain Halvar comes down for a drink one night a month. That's the night to find him talkative.

npcs.name: Captain Vesna
npcs.title: Gate Captain
npcs.personality: Severe, observant, scars on her forearms. Doesn't waste words.
lore: I log every traveler in and out. Last week six went north. Three came back. Make of that what you will.
Don't leave the gate without a weapon. Bandits, wolves, and worse, down the Stoneveil road.
The watchtower's a fifteen-minute walk north. Captain Halvar's been there since before I was born.
If you've never been north of the gate, take it slow. Every step you take, the DM weaves the country a little wider.
Old man Aldric's orchard is south, not north — and even there, there's been talk of trouble lately.

npcs.name: Captain Halvar
npcs.title: Watchtower Captain
npcs.personality: Aging, sharp-eyed, with stories of beasts and battles in equal measure. Glad to talk if you bring news from the south.

routines.activity: scanning the horizon from the parapet

lore: Three nights running I've seen lights up at the old hill shrine. Lights that don't behave like fire and don't behave like men.
There's a deer-trail north of here that climbs into the foothills. It used to lead to the shrine. Walk it with care.
I had an apprentice once — Pell. Old John's lad. Went up the trail last spring. Never saw him come back down.
The Frostpeak mountains far to the north used to host dragons. Used to. Lately I'm not so sure they've stayed gone.
If you head north and need a place to sleep, double back to Silverbrook before dark. Whatever's up there, it walks at night.
